text.title: PLG Metrics Core Rules
text.summary: 5 testable rules every application of `PLG Metrics` must enforce. Each rule pairs a binary check with a sourced rationale.

rule.statement: Each PLG metric MUST have a pinned formula + named source events + cohort definition. Hand-wave definitions are forbidden.
rule.rationale: Unpinned definitions cause dashboard drift; the pinned formula keeps quarterly reports comparable.

rule.statement: Each metric MUST cite a target band with source (industry benchmark + year). 'Higher is better' is forbidden as a target.
rule.rationale: Without a target, the team optimises forever; the band + source anchor decision-making.

rule.statement: Each metric MUST have a single named owner accountable for moving it. Group ownership is forbidden.
rule.rationale: Group-owned metrics drift; named owner has accountability + lever to act.

rule.statement: Each cohort-based metric MUST pin its cohort definition (signup week / month, segment, region). Mixing cohort windows is forbidden.
rule.rationale: Mixed cohorts mislead diagnostics (e.g. mixing weekly + monthly retention curves); pinning enforces apples-to-apples comparisons.

rule.statement: Each claim about a metric (lift, baseline, target) MUST link to a dashboard row + timestamp. Un-anchored claims are demoted to hypotheses.
rule.rationale: Un-anchored claims invite re-litigation; dashboard links keep the data behind every claim.

rule.statement: When preconditions in `Applies If` fail OR any item in `Skip If` triggers, do not run this methodology. Document the skip rationale in the artefact's parent log.
rule.rationale: Misapplied methodologies are worse than no methodology — they consume review cycles and produce false confidence.
